MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Amend To File Second Amended Complaint For Fraud, Conspiracy To Commit Fraud, Declaratory Relief, Breach Of Fiduciary Duty Conversion And Declaratory Relief And Specific Performance Seeking Order Compelling Election Of Directors Of Jones Memorial Homes, Inc. And Related Matters
Off Calendar Jun-30-2017 Continued To Aug-29-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
T. Scott Tate, Esq. (415-36-6719)of Schnaders Attorneys for Plaintiff.
Dolores Bastian Dalton, Esq. (51-836-6336) of Goldfarb Lipman Attorneys for Defendant.

RULING - ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling with modification as follows:
Plaintiffs hearing on Revised Second Amended complaint is continued to August 29, 2017 as well as the Defendant's Motion for Protective order and Plaintiff's Motion to compel will all be heard on the same day at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302. PLAINTIFFS PAMELA KELLY and JOHNNY JOHNS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Second Amended Complaint For Fraud, Conspiracy To Commit Fraud, Declaratory Relief, Breach Of Fiduciary Duty Conversion And Declaratory Relief is DENIED in substantial part. Plaintiffs are given leave to file a second amended complaint that alleges the sixth (mislabeled as the fifth) cause of action for declaratory relief and request for order appointing directors for Jones Homes and Jones Senior Homes. All other aspects of plaintiffs' motion for leave to file a second amended complaint are denied. (The proposed second amended complaint contains a typographical error by labeling two different claims as the third cause of action (pp. 16 and 17). The court refers to the second of the two causes of action labeled the third cause of action as the fourth cause of action, the cause of action labeled the fourth cause of action as the fifth cause of action, and the cause of action labeled as the fifth cause of action as the sixth cause of action.) While amendments to pleadings are liberally granted, the proposed second amended complaint seeks to re-litigate claims and issues that have already been explicitly or necessarily decided adversely to plaintiffs and their privity, the Jones Memorial United Methodist Church, by the Court of Appeal in the decision filed on January 6, 2017 in A145711 and by Judge Robertson in this case in the orders filed February 4, 2015 and March 25, 2015. Plaintiffs are in privity with the Church because all of the claims and rights that plaintiffs assert are based on their membership in the Church and plaintiffs have the identical interests as those asserted by the Church. The Court of Appeal decision recognizes this point by, among other things, stating that the "gravamen of the Church's action is that defendants wrongfully excluded Church members from membership in Homes and Senior Homes" (p. 7), which is also a fair characterization of this case, including the first five causes of action in the proposed second amended complaint. Both the Court of Appeal decision and Judge Robertson's orders establish the lack of merit to the first five causes of action in the proposed first amended complaint. The portion of the Court of Appeal's decision holding that the Church was on inquiry notice of the defendants' wrongful exclusion of its members in 2007 (p. 8) bars the first two causes of action in the proposed second amended complaint. The portion of the Court of Appeal's decision rejecting the argument that the Church's members are also members of Homes and Senior Homes (p. 10) bars the first five causes of action in the proposed second amended complaint. Judge Robertson's orders have already determined the lack of merit of the first five causes of action and there is no reason why plaintiffs should be permitted to re-litigate those issues. The sixth cause of action in the proposed second amended complaint has not been explicitly or necessarily addressed in the Court of Appeal's decision or Judge Robertson's orders and thus plaintiffs are granted leave to allege that claim. (see word minutes for further details, hearing date and briefing schedule) (as fully reported on the reporter's notes.) Order and stipulation are signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. T. SCOTT TATE OF THE SCHNADER, HARRISON, SEGAL & LEWIS LLP, TEL. NO. 364-6719 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DOLORES BASTIAN DALTON & ATTY. CELIA W. LEE OF THE GOLDFARB LIPMAN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (510)836-6336 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; Plaintiffs Johnny Johns, Pamela Kelley, And Pamela Kelly's Petition For Order Setting Elections For Board Of Directors Of Jones Memorial Homes, Inc. And Determining Membership Eligible To Vote is Denied. First, the evidence indicates that defendant's bylaws were amended in 2006 and defendant is no longer a member corporation. Second, even if those Bylaws are not effective and defendant is a member corporation, Petitioners lack standing to maintain this action under CA Corporation Code section 5515.
Petitioners cannot maintain this action because they are not "a director, officer, delegate, member or the Attorney General within the meaning of the statute. In addition, the Board of Directors is vested with authority to determine membership issues and has exercised that discretion against petitioners. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ar
